verdict_reasoning: KLK2 (hK2) is a secreted serine protease of the kallikrein family. It is synthesized with a signal peptide and pro-peptide, then secreted into the extracellular milieu after activation by cleavage; there is no transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, or outer-leaflet lipidation. The protein is found in seminal plasma and serum, not stably anchored to any cell surface. Pre-`no` checklist: (1) no cell-surface-directed antibody/ADC/CAR-T targeting the cell-bound form — serum-based diagnostics target the secreted pool; (2) not a sheddable TM precursor — purely secreted with signal peptide only; (3) no covalent/wash-resistant TM-partner tethering documented; (4) no documented minority PM pool or cycling; (5) no cancer/cell-death/activation ecto-display, no germline-restricted surface display, no lysosomal exocytosis context reported. The kallikrein gene-group is a secreted-serine-protease family, consistent with secreted_only.
